description: Reference solutions for training exercises in ../exercises.json

ex01_seed_expansion_basics.question: Does a 0.01 perturbation to seed[0] produce measurable output change?
ex01_seed_expansion_basics.insight: The mapping is deterministic and sensitive to small seed changes

ex02_noise_sensitivity.question: How does SNR scale with noise amplitude?
ex02_noise_sensitivity.insight: Matched filtering provides ~sqrt(N) SNR improvement over threshold detection

ex03_detection_threshold.question: What is the minimum delta-V for reliable detection?
ex03_detection_threshold.answer: Depends on noise floor and Pfa target
ex03_detection_threshold.insight: Detection probability rises sharply once SNR exceeds ~10 dB

ex04_constellation_geometry.question: Why do prime harmonic ratios minimize channel cross-talk?
ex04_constellation_geometry.answer: Co-prime periods have maximum LCM, so satellites revisit the same relative geometry only after many orbits
ex04_constellation_geometry.insight: Prime ratios produce longer repeat periods = more distinct phase combinations

ex05_end_to_end_pipeline.question: Can a seed be recovered from its own clean output?
ex05_end_to_end_pipeline.bottleneck: Recovery degrades rapidly with noise; codebook approaches are the next step
ex05_end_to_end_pipeline.insight: Seed recovery is the bottleneck, not detection or encoding
